channel.title: w3srss
channel.description: subscribe to my rss feed for latest updates.

item.title: ADB - L33T commands ADB - L33t commands
item.description:
adb shell screenrecord

adb shell screenrecord /sdcard/test.mp4

Records the device's display to a .mp4 file

adb pull /sdcard/test.mp4

Download test.mp4 file from Android Device.

adb shell screenrecord [options] 

Options:


--size WIDTHxHEIGHT

  Set the video size, e.g. "1280x720". Default is the device's main

  display resolution (if supported), 1280x720 if not. For best results,

  use a size supported by the AVC encoder.



--bit-rate RATE

  Set the video bit rate, in bits per second. Value may be specified as

  bits or megabits, e.g. '4000000' is equivalent to '4M'. Default 20Mbps.



--bugreport

  Add additional information, such as a timestamp overlay, that is helpful

  in videos captured to illustrate bugs.



--time-limit TIME

  Set the maximum recording time, in seconds. Default / maximum is 180.



--verbose

  Display interesting information on stdout.

item.title: Using ADB to automate dating app swipes 🏂
item.description:
🔮

We've all tried dating apps even if it's with a psudo account. Most of them now emulate one common structure in which the app interacts with the user. After successfull sacrifice of your personal information , you will be required to swipe left / right, tap on a love icon if a profile you've come across peaks your interest :-) . And cross or vice versa if you don't like a profile. Due to desperation or 'time' almost everyone swipes right on any profile quickly to pass the whole reading a profile's bio and shit like that. What if you got a badoo premium subscription for example, which goes for a week and want to maximise the 'unlimited' swipes you get after paying $1 read on fellow soul surfer .

Requirements for all the magic :

  • A device running the Android Operating System / Android Emulator ( if you love your privacy )
  • ADB tools / android-tools installed on your linux / windows / osx node
  • Yeah basic linux commands and navigation skills is also required. Google your bugs , it works on my side 🤖
  • Some brainzzz
